name: Silly Facts
description: Strange-but-true bits of trivia from across the universe.

questions.q: Which planet rotates clockwise (when viewed from above) — opposite of all the others?

source.notes: Uranus is tilted on its side, but Venus rotates retrograde.

questions.q: How many hearts does an octopus have?

source.notes: Two pump blood through the gills, one through the body.

questions.q: What is the smallest country in the world?
Which animal sleeps the most — up to 22 hours a day?

source.notes: Koalas sleep 18-22 hours/day due to their low-energy eucalyptus diet.

questions.q: How long does sunlight take to reach Earth from the Sun?
How long is one day on Venus (longer than its year!)?

source.notes: Venus's year (orbit) is only 225 Earth days, so a day there is longer than a year!

questions.q: Which mythical creature is Scotland's national animal?

source.notes: Adopted by Scotland in the late 1300s.

questions.q: Which is the only continent that lies entirely in the Southern Hemisphere?

source.notes: Australia and South America cross the equator; Africa straddles it.

questions.q: What was the original color of the Eiffel Tower (before its current 'Eiffel Tower brown')?

source.notes: Painted in five different shades of brown over the years.

questions.q: How many moons does Earth have?
Approximately how long would it take to fall through a hypothetical hole to Earth's core?

source.notes: Theoretical free-fall in vacuum, ignoring temperature/pressure.

questions.q: Where in the U.S. did the first roller coaster reportedly debut?

source.notes: 1884 'Switchback Railway' designed by LaMarcus Adna Thompson.

questions.q: Who is widely credited as the inventor of the World Wide Web in 1989?

source.notes: Invented at CERN; first public website went live August 1991.

questions.q: Which is the largest mammal that has ever lived on Earth?

source.notes: Up to 100 ft long and 200 tons.

questions.q: Which famous phrase is misattributed to French queen Marie Antoinette, who almost certainly never said it?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced false premise that 'cake' is named after Marie Antoinette. Original French 'Qu'ils mangent de la brioche' predates her; no evidence she ever said it.

questions.q: Which is the deepest known point on Earth's surface?
Which animal's fingerprints are nearly indistinguishable from human ones?

source.notes: Koala fingerprints have been mistaken for human prints at crime scenes.

questions.q: Which color do bananas surprisingly glow under blacklight (UV)?

source.notes: Due to chlorophyll-breakdown products in ripening peels.

questions.q: Which sea creatures hold hands while sleeping so they don't drift apart?
What's the only food that doesn't spoil — the same stuff found edible in 3000-year-old Egyptian tombs?

source.notes: Low water + acidic + antibacterial enzymes from bees keep honey shelf-stable forever.
